title: Automata Studio
desc: Build and simulate DFAs, NFAs, GNFAs, PDAs, Turing Machines, and CFGs in the browser. Convert between representations — NFA→DFA, DFA minimisation, GNFA→regex, regex→NFA, and CFG→CNF — with step-by-step visualisation and LaTeX export.

title: Turing Machine Simulator
desc: Write transition rules in a plain-text format, load a tape, and step through the universal model of computation. Includes a 2D mode whose star feature is a 2D→1D compiler: any 2D Turing program is mechanically rewritten into an equivalent 1D program that lays each row out between separators and shuffles head markers around — a constructive proof that the extra dimension buys you no extra power.

title: Game of Life
desc: Conway’s classic cellular automaton on a configurable grid. Draw patterns, run generations, and export the board state directly into the 2D Turing Machine simulator.

title: Typing Tester PEG Grammar
desc: Practise typing Python at speed. A weighted PEG-grammar generator builds plausible Python snippets — assignments, if/else, for loops, function and class definitions — and you race against either a time limit or a word limit. Live WPM and accuracy, easy/normal/hard difficulty, and results saved in local storage with a progress-over-time chart.

title: Boids
desc: Craig Reynolds’ classic emergent flocking algorithm from 1987. Three local forces — separation, alignment, cohesion — produce complex collective motion from zero central coordination.

title: Tic Tac Toe
desc: Four variants on the classic 3×3 grid — a perfect minimax AI, an easy one-ply opponent, a handicapped baby-mode, and a local two-player mode. Pick your difficulty.
